codeReview.summary: The scaffold covers health/SSE/config basics and test harness behavior, but it misses key hosted-runtime requirements and is not fully production-stable for remote use.

issues.description: Server startup hard-binds to 127.0.0.1 (`httpServer.listen(config.port, '127.0.0.1', ...)`). For a hosted relay runtime, this prevents external/container ingress and breaks remote feature accessibility.
Feature description requires persistence bootstrap, but the entrypoint only loads config and starts the HTTP server (`loadRelayConfig` + `createRelayServer`) with no persistence initialization path.
`relay:dev` runs via `npx tsx`, but `tsx` is not declared in devDependencies. This makes startup depend on implicit global cache/network availability and reduces determinism.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance describes a hosted relay API/SSE scope but does not explicitly document interface-binding expectations (e.g., 0.0.0.0 vs 127.0.0.1) for deployable relay services.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/justen/.factory/missions/54bd84c5-2680-4f00-890c-9c832860b3d4/AGENTS.md:6 states 'hosted relay API/SSE'; reviewed commit binds listener at src/relay/server.ts:128 to 127.0.0.1.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared environment docs/services do not call out the runtime dependency implied by `relay:dev` using `npx tsx`, which can require network/global cache on fresh environments.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json:18 sets relay:dev to `npx tsx src/relay/index.ts`; package.json:24+ has no `tsx` dependency; .factory/library/environment.md runtime baseline (lines 10-13) does not mention tsx requirement.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit e18169d. Health endpoint/SSE/config diagnostics and tests were implemented, but the feature fails review due to loopback-only binding and missing persistence bootstrap relative to feature scope.
